items.question_en: Which RE tasks are reported in the papers?
items.notes: Simple task-framing lookup over the RE task field.

items.question_en: For each RE task, which NLP task types are reported?
items.notes: Links the problem framing to the nested NLP task-type structure.

items.question_en: Which papers report both an NLP task input and an NLP task output type, and what are they?
items.notes: Requires traversing both child and grandchild task fields in the same item.

items.question_en: Which papers report an NLP task output classification label, and which output types accompany it?
items.notes: Uses the output subtemplate to retrieve a task-specific classification label.

items.question_en: Which papers report an extracted element in the NLP task output, and which output types are associated with it?
items.notes: Another output-subtemplate query that focuses on information-extraction style outputs.

items.question_en: Which publication years contain papers that report an NLP task output type together with a translation mapping cardinality, and how many such papers are there per year?
items.notes: Combines nested task-output traversal with paper-year grouping.

items.question_en: Which papers report both a venue and an RE task?
items.notes: Connects contribution-level venue metadata with the reported research problem.

items.question_en: Which papers report both an NLP dataset and an annotation process?
items.notes: Checks whether two major contribution substructures are both present.

items.question_en: Which papers report an RE task, an NLP task type, and an evaluation metric together?
items.notes: Bridges the problem, task, and evaluation sections in one contribution-level query.

items.question_en: Which papers report either an annotation process or an evaluation, but not both?
items.notes: Uses an exclusive either-or pattern over two top-level contribution branches.

items.question_en: Which venues contain papers that report the same combination of RE task and NLP task type?
items.notes: Summarizes contribution content by venue and task framing.

items.question_en: Which papers report both an NLP dataset and an implemented approach but do not report an evaluation?
items.notes: Captures a realistic missing-evaluation pattern over otherwise rich contribution records.

items.question_en: Which papers report an implemented approach type?
items.notes: Simple methodology lookup over implemented approach types.

items.question_en: Which algorithms are reported for the implemented approaches in each paper?
items.notes: Directly retrieves algorithm choices from the implemented approach subtree.

items.question_en: Which papers report both an algorithm and a running requirement for the implemented approach?
items.notes: Combines two sibling methodology properties under implemented approach.

items.question_en: Which approach types are reported together with which documentation types and dependencies?
items.notes: Uses a three-way methodology combination inside the same approach object.

items.question_en: Which papers report at least one algorithm for the implemented approach but no approach type?
items.notes: Classic missing-information test over two closely related methodology fields.

items.question_en: Which papers report a release for the implemented approach but no methodology license type?
items.notes: Requires the release subtemplate and a negative check on the approach-level license.

items.question_en: Which papers report at least two different algorithms for the same implemented approach?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT ?paper ?paperLabel
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?algorithmLabel; SEPARATOR=" | ") AS ?algorithms)
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:P181046 ?implementedApproach .
  ?implementedApproach orkgp:P58069 ?algorithm .
  OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel . }
  OPTIONAL { ?algorithm rdfs:label ?algorithmLabel . }
}
GROUP BY ?paper ?paperLabel
HAVING (COUNT(DISTINCT ?algorithm) >= 2)
ORDER BY ?paperLabel
items.notes: Uses grouping and HAVING to detect richer implemented approaches.

items.question_en: Which combinations of approach type and algorithm are reported most frequently, and which papers belong to each combination?
items.notes: A genuinely hard methodology query with subqueries, aggregation, and ranking.

items.question_en: Which NLP datasets are used in the papers?
items.notes: Basic dataset lookup from the top-level dataset field.

items.question_en: Which natural languages are reported for the NLP datasets in each paper?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?paper ?paperLabel ?naturalLanguage ?naturalLanguageLabel
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:P181011 ?dataset .
  ?dataset orkgp:P181022 ?dataType .
  ?dataType orkgp:P181025 ?naturalLanguage .
  OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel . }
  OPTIONAL { ?naturalLanguage rdfs:label ?naturalLanguageLabel . }
}
ORDER BY ?paperLabel ?naturalLanguageLabel
items.notes: Traverses the dataset → data type → natural language path.

items.question_en: Which papers report both an NLP data source type and a data source domain, and what are they?
items.notes: Uses two nested data-source properties from the dataset subtree.

items.question_en: Which papers report a dataset URL together with public availability, license type, and dataset location type?
items.notes: Combines license and location subtemplates under the dataset branch.

items.question_en: Which papers report at least one NLP data source type but no data source domain?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?paper ?paperLabel
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:P181011 ?dataset .
  ?dataset orkgp:P181017 ?dataSource .
  ?dataSource orkgp:P181018 ?sourceType .
  FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?dataSource orkgp:P181020 ?sourceDomain . }
  OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel . }
}
ORDER BY ?paperLabel
items.notes: Tests missing nested source metadata while requiring a present source type.

items.question_en: Which papers report a dataset URL but no dataset location type?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?paper ?paperLabel
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:P181011 ?dataset .
  ?dataset orkgp:P181028 ?datasetLocation .
  ?datasetLocation orkgp:P1003 ?url .
  FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?datasetLocation orkgp:P181030 ?locationType . }
  OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel . }
}
ORDER BY ?paperLabel
items.notes: Uses absence of a child triple inside the dataset-location subtemplate.

items.question_en: For each paper, which combinations of NLP data format and natural language are reported?
items.notes: Aggregates two data-type properties into per-paper combinations.

items.question_en: Which papers report both the number of data sources and the number of data items for the NLP dataset?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?paper ?paperLabel ?numberOfSources ?dataItem
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:P181011 ?dataset .
  ?dataset orkgp:P181015 ?dataItem ;
           orkgp:P181017 ?dataSource .
  ?dataSource orkgp:P181019 ?numberOfSources .
  OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel . }
}
ORDER BY ?paperLabel ?numberOfSources ?dataItem
items.notes: Pairs two quantitative-looking dataset properties from different nesting levels.

items.question_en: Which papers report an annotation scheme together with a guideline availability value?
items.notes: Captures a common annotation-resource path through the annotation scheme subtemplate.

items.question_en: Which papers report annotator agreement but do not report any conflict resolution method?
items.notes: Uses the annotation-agreement subtemplate with a schema-faithful missing child field.

items.question_en: Which evaluation metrics are reported in each paper?
items.notes: Basic evaluation lookup over reported metrics.

items.question_en: Which papers report both an evaluation metric and a validation procedure, and what are they?
items.notes: Pairs the two main sibling properties inside the evaluation object.

items.question_en: Which papers report baseline comparison types in the evaluation, and which types are they?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?paper ?paperLabel ?baselineType ?baselineTypeLabel
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:HAS_EVALUATION ?evaluation .
  ?evaluation orkgp:P181051 ?baselineComparison .
  ?baselineComparison orkgp:P181052 ?baselineType .
  OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel . }
  OPTIONAL { ?baselineType rdfs:label ?baselineTypeLabel . }
}
ORDER BY ?paperLabel ?baselineTypeLabel
items.notes: Traverses evaluation into the baseline-comparison subtemplate.

items.question_en: Which papers report a baseline comparison but no baseline comparison detail?
items.notes: Checks for missing detail within the baseline-comparison subtree.

items.question_en: Which papers report an evaluation metric but no validation procedure?
items.notes: A useful missing-information pattern inside the evaluation subtemplate.

items.question_en: Do any papers report both an evaluation metric and a baseline comparison type in the same evaluation?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

ASK {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:HAS_EVALUATION ?evaluation .
  ?evaluation orkgp:P110006 ?metric ;
              orkgp:P181051 ?baselineComparison .
  ?baselineComparison orkgp:P181052 ?baselineType .
}
items.notes: A boolean existence query over linked evaluation and baseline fields.

items.question_en: Which pairs of evaluation metric and validation procedure are reported most frequently, and which papers use each pair?
items.notes: Hard metric-focused ranking query with global maxima and grouped paper lists.

items.question_en: Which papers report release formats for the implemented approach?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?paper ?paperLabel ?releaseFormat ?releaseFormatLabel
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:P181046 ?implementedApproach .
  ?implementedApproach orkgp:release ?release .
  ?release orkgp:P181049 ?releaseFormat .
  OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel . }
  OPTIONAL { ?releaseFormat rdfs:label ?releaseFormatLabel . }
}
ORDER BY ?paperLabel ?releaseFormatLabel
items.notes: Basic resource query over the release subtemplate of the implemented approach.

items.question_en: Which papers report release URLs together with documentation types?
items.notes: Combines sibling resource metadata with the nested release URL.

items.question_en: Which papers report both dependencies and running requirements for the implemented approach?
items.notes: Captures operational resource requirements at methodology level.

items.question_en: Which papers report a release format or a release location type, but no release URL?
items.notes: Uses a release-level UNION with a missing-URL constraint.

items.question_en: Which papers provide documentation for the implemented approach but no release information?
items.notes: Contrasts provided documentation against absent release metadata.

items.question_en: Which combinations of RE task and NLP task type are shared by at least two papers, and which papers belong to each combination?
items.notes: Compares papers by shared problem-and-task-type combinations.

items.question_en: Which combinations of NLP data source type and evaluation metric are shared by at least two papers?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT ?sourceType ?sourceTypeLabel ?metric ?metricLabel
       (COUNT(DISTINCT ?paper) AS ?paperCount)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?paperLabel; SEPARATOR=" | ") AS ?papers)
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:P181011 ?dataset ;
                orkgp:HAS_EVALUATION ?evaluation .
  ?dataset orkgp:P181017 ?dataSource .
  ?dataSource orkgp:P181018 ?sourceType .
  ?evaluation orkgp:P110006 ?metric .
  OPTIONAL { ?paper rdfs:label ?paperLabel . }
  OPTIONAL { ?sourceType rdfs:label ?sourceTypeLabel . }
  OPTIONAL { ?metric rdfs:label ?metricLabel . }
}
GROUP BY ?sourceType ?sourceTypeLabel ?metric ?metricLabel
HAVING (COUNT(DISTINCT ?paper) >= 2)
ORDER BY DESC(?paperCount) ?sourceTypeLabel ?metricLabel
items.notes: Cross-compares data and evaluation choices across the corpus.

items.question_en: Which papers report either an algorithm or a dependency together with a release format, and which kind of methodology evidence do they provide?
items.notes: Uses UNION to compare two different methodology evidence paths under the same release condition.

items.question_en: Which approach types are paired with which evaluation metrics, and which papers report each pairing?
items.notes: Compares methodology and evaluation choices at the pairing level.

items.question_en: Which papers report both the natural language and the data format of the NLP data, and which papers report only one of the two?
items.notes: Explicitly compares complete versus partial reporting of two sibling dataset properties.

items.question_en: Which combinations of guideline availability and shared material are shared by at least two papers?
items.notes: Compares annotation-support configurations across papers.

items.question_en: How many papers are reported for each RE task?
items.sparql:
PREFIX orkgr: <http://orkg.org/orkg/resource/>
PREFIX orkgc: <http://orkg.org/orkg/class/>
PREFIX orkgp: <http://orkg.org/orkg/predicate/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT ?reTask ?reTaskLabel (COUNT(DISTINCT ?paper) AS ?paperCount)
WHERE {
  ?paper orkgp:P31 ?contribution .
  ?contribution a orkgc:C121001 ;
                orkgp:P181002 ?reTask .
  OPTIONAL { ?reTask rdfs:label ?reTaskLabel . }
}
GROUP BY ?reTask ?reTaskLabel
ORDER BY DESC(?paperCount) ?reTaskLabel
items.notes: Simple corpus-level count grouped by RE task.

items.question_en: Which publication years contain the largest number of papers that report both an evaluation metric and a validation procedure?
items.notes: Hard aggregate query with year grouping and a global-maximum subquery.
